title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Dueling AI agents could reveal keys to restoring consciousness
items.description: A new study probes consciousness with models that simulate and classify brain activity. A neuroscientist behind the work explains its ambitions

items.title: Neanderthals survived on a knife’s edge for 350,000 years
items.description: A pair of studies illuminates these humans’ long, hardscrabble existence

items.title: AI algorithms can become ‘agents of chaos’
items.description: Given autonomous control of other software, programs shared private medical details and deleted files without permission

items.title: Iran’s internet shutdown muddies global efforts to track damage to historical sites
items.description: At least 56 cultural sites have been damaged since strikes began in February—but internet blackouts and academic isolation are obscuring the true toll

items.title: Hormone linked to morning sickness may help reduce alcohol intake
items.description: GDF15, which is thought to contribute to nausea during pregnancy, may keep our drinking in check, researchers propose

items.title: Microscopic spikes on snakeskin block bacterial buildup
items.description: Structures on python scales could inspire chemical-free antimicrobial designs

items.title: First science from private Moon lander challenges lunar divide
items.description: Blue Ghost data suggest NASA’s growing commercial Moon program can deliver results

items.title: Energy Department merges nuclear and particle physics programs, unsettling researchers
items.description: In spite of synergies, physicists worry both fields are being sidelined in favor of AI

items.title: How realistic is the science in Project Hail Mary?
items.description: From the viability of waterless life to how researchers should handle cosmic emergencies, astronomer Wendy Freedman weighs in on the new sci-fi film

items.title: What happened when an Arab neuroscientist took the helm at an Israeli university?
items.description: Mouna Maroun’s stewardship highlights the promise—and perils—of reconciliation through science
